description: Authoritative state of known ME/CFS drug trials in Germany. Updated by the routine in ROUTINE.md. The first run after setup will (correctly) flag every relevant trial it finds as 'new', because this baseline starts empty. Do not hand-fabricate entries — only add trials confirmed from a registry or institutional source during a check.

trials.name: Observational Study on Immunoadsorption (IA) in Patients With Autoantibody-Positive Post-Infectious ME/CFS

intervention.name: Immunoadsorption (TheraSorb®)
intervention.mechanism: Removes GPCR autoantibodies (adrenergic, antineuronal) from blood via TheraSorb® affinity column (Miltenyi); 5 outpatient sessions over 2 weeks.

trials.sponsor: Charité University, Berlin (supported by German Federal Ministry of Research and Weidenhammer-Zöbele Foundation)

sites.institution: Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Institute for Medical Immunology

eligibility.summary: Adults 18–65 with post-infectious ME/CFS (Canadian Consensus Criteria), Bell Disability Score <60, post-exertional malaise ≥14 h, and detectable autoantibodies (adrenergic or antineuronal).
key_inclusion: ME/CFS per Canadian Consensus Criteria with PEM ≥14 h
key_exclusion: Conditions that preclude definite ME/CFS diagnosis

trials.notes: Immunoadsorption is apheresis (borderline for drug/pharmacological criterion) — included per ROUTINE.md: 'immunoadsorption is borderline → include + note it'; is_drug_study set to false. Status verified recruiting on ClinicalTrials.gov (April 2026). Contact: Dr. Elisa A Stein, elisa.stein@charite.de, +49 450 624354. Successor to the earlier Trial RIA (NCT05629988).

trials.name: MDC002 Phase 1 Clinical Trial in ME/CFS (planned)

intervention.name: MDC002
intervention.mechanism: Orally applicable small molecule; stimulates Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase and mitochondrial Na⁺/Ca²⁺ exchanger NCLX in skeletal muscle to interrupt calcium-overload cycle and restore cellular energy production.

trials.condition: ME/CFS, post-acute infection syndromes with exertional intolerance / PEM
trials.phase: Phase 1 (planned; pre-clinical stage as of mid-2026)

eligibility.summary: To be defined when trial opens; clinical trials are in planning stage as of June 2026.

trials.notes: Not yet registered in any trial registry. German criterion satisfied by German sponsor (Mitodicure GmbH). Pipeline page (mitodicure.com/pipeline/) confirms MDC002 is 'ready for GLP toxicity and GLP safety pharmacology studies' as of June 2026 — i.e. still pre-clinical; no Phase 1 registration or new funding announcement. Watch for Phase 1 registration on DRKS or ClinicalTrials.gov.

trials.name: Observational study on immune adsorption (IA) in patients with GPCR autoantibody-positive post-COVID/post-vac syndrome (IA-Surv)

intervention.name: Immunoadsorption (IA apheresis)
intervention.mechanism: 5 immunoadsorption apheresis sessions targeting GPCR autoantibodies; outcomes assessed at baseline, after 5th session, and at 6, 12, and 24 weeks post-treatment.

trials.sponsor: Universitätsmedizin Charité Berlin (institutional budget, no external funding)
trials.principal_investigator: Prof. Dr. med. Harald Matthes, Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin
trials.condition: Post-COVID-19/post-vaccination syndrome with ME/CFS and GPCR autoantibodies

eligibility.summary: Adults 18–75 with post-COVID or post-vaccination syndrome lasting ≥6 months, positive GPCR autoantibodies, and valid German health insurance.
key_inclusion: Post-COVID or post-vaccination syndrome lasting ≥6 months since infection/vaccination

trials.notes: Immunoadsorption is apheresis (borderline for drug/pharmacological criterion) — included per ROUTINE.md: 'immunoadsorption is borderline → include + note it'; is_drug_study set to false. Registered DRKS January 27, 2025; actual start January 23, 2025; planned completion December 31, 2026. Target n=82. PI is Prof. Matthes at Gemeinschaftskrankenhaus Havelhöhe (Berlin), not directly affiliated with Scheibenbogen's IMPACT trial (NCT07529197) — different PI and sites, though both use IA apheresis for GPCR autoantibody-positive post-infectious conditions. Confirmed recruiting on DRKS 2026-06-27.

trials.name: Randomized Adaptive Assessment of Post-COVID Syndrome Treatments — REVIVE arm (vidofludimus calcium)

intervention.name: Vidofludimus calcium (IMU-838)
intervention.mechanism: Oral selective DHODH inhibitor with anti-inflammatory and antiviral activity (also activates the neuroprotective transcription factor Nurr1). Dosing 22.5 mg/day for 7 days, then 45 mg/day for 49 days, vs placebo. Rationale: reduce inflammatory activity in Post-COVID Syndrome.

trials.sponsor: Goethe University Frankfurt (Prof. Dr. Maria J. G. T. Vehreschild); part of the NAPKON / Netzwerk Universitätsmedizin (NUM) post-COVID platform, BMBF-funded, co-financed by a pharmaceutical manufacturer.
trials.principal_investigator: Prof. Dr. Maria J. G. T. Vehreschild (Infectiology, University Hospital Frankfurt); trial coordination University Medical Center Göttingen
trials.condition: Post-COVID Syndrome (PCS) with moderate–severe disability — overlaps the post-COVID ME/CFS population (fatigue, PEM-adjacent exertional intolerance, cognitive and orthostatic symptoms)
trials.phase: Phase 2 (double-blind, placebo-controlled, 1:1 response-adaptive randomised platform arm; n≈376 across 11 German sites)

eligibility.summary: Adults ≥18 with Post-COVID Syndrome persisting >12 weeks after COVID-19, moderate–severe disability (Bell Disability Scale 20–60), and ≥2 PCS symptoms of moderate/severe intensity (e.g. fatigue FSS ≥36, cognitive impairment MoCA 10–25, dyspnea mMRC ≥2, or orthostatic dysfunction).
key_inclusion: Post-COVID Syndrome symptoms persisting >12 weeks after COVID-19
≥2 PCS symptoms incl. fatigue (FSS ≥36), cognitive impairment (MoCA 10–25), dyspnea (mMRC ≥2), or orthostatic dysfunction

trials.notes: Added 2026-06-30 after this trial was found to have been missed by earlier runs. ROOT CAUSE of the miss: registry searches used only ME/CFS-specific terms (ME/CFS, chronisches Fatigue-Syndrom, myalgische Enzephalomyelitis, post-exertional) and never 'Post-COVID-Syndrom'/'Long COVID', and the NAPKON/NUM post-COVID platform was not a monitored source — so a post-COVID drug RCT (even one with a Charité site) was invisible. Targets PCS broadly, not ME/CFS-exclusively, but eligibility (Bell 20–60, fatigue/PEM-adjacent, orthostatic) heavily overlaps post-COVID ME/CFS, which is explicitly in scope. RAPID is the adaptive platform; REVIVE is its first intervention-specific arm ('reducing inflammatory activity in PCS'). Confirmed via CTIS 2024-511628-16-00 and the published protocol (PMC12366011); first patient enrolled Sep 2024; ~376 patients, 11 German sites incl. Charité Berlin (Prof. Witzenrath). Not a Wirth/Scheibenbogen trial (priority normal).
